title: Bad C++ (error: ‘vlSelf’ was not declared in this scope)
body:
Thanks for taking the time to report this.

Can you attach an example that shows the issue?  (Must be openly licensed, completely self-contained and self-checking so can directly run what you provide. Ideally use test_regress format, see https://veripool.org/guide/latest/contributing.html?highlight=test_regress#reporting-bugs

> Working on it, sadly in another big codebase top level test.

What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?

> `verilator --binary --timing --assert --autoflush -j 2 -sv -Wno-CASEINCOMPLETE -Wno-REALCVT -Wno-SELRANGE -Wno-TIMESCALEMOD -Wno-UNSIGNED -Wno-WIDTH -CFLAGS -O0 --unroll-count 4 -top XX_YY_test -o sim.exe <defines, incdirs, files>`



What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?

> Fails on today's master: `Verilator 5.035 devel rev v5.034-154-g6bb57e463`
> Works on `Verilator 5.034 2025-02-24 rev v5.034`

What OS and distribution are you using?

> Ubuntu22.04

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?

Sure. 

(Please avoid attaching screenshots that show text - you can convert images to text using e.g. https://ocr.space)


My actual (somewhat obfuscated) message looks like this:

```

VXX_YY_test___024root__DepSet_hc51b8287__0.cpp:172:9: error: ‘vlSelf’ was not declared in this scope
  172 |         vlSelf->__Vtask_XX_YY_test__DOT__uDUT__DOT__LongHierPath__unnamedblk1_1__DOT____Vrepeat0 = VL_RAND_RESET_I(32);
      |         ^~~~~~
VXX_YY_test___024root__DepSet_hc51b8287__0.cpp: In member function ‘VlCoroutine VXX_YY_test___024root::__VnoInFunc_XX_YY_test__DOT__UserCsrRead32_TOP(VXX_YY_test__Syms*, IData, IData&, IData)’:
VXX_YY_test___024root__DepSet_hc51b8287__0.cpp:1416:9: error: ‘vlSelf’ was not declared in this scope
 1416 |         vlSelf->__Vtask_XX_YY_test__DOT__uDUT__DOT__LongHierPath__unnamedblk1_4__DOT____Vrepeat3 = VL_RAND_RESET_I(32);
      |         ^~~~~~


```

I noticed in 5.034 (working version for me) most of these vlCoroutine .cpp functions have *vlSelf as an arg, and it's missing for some reason in 5.035. I'm not sure where to even start on this, it's a part of Verilator's src I have never explored. 

This could also be an obscure SV coding style issue on my end. Not all of these involve VL_RAND_RESET_I(32), and I didn't see a verilator knob for random reset values (I saw a runtime knob on the verialted exe, but we're not getting that far w/ bad .cpp).
code_blocks:

  172 |         vlSelf->__Vtask_XX_YY_test__DOT__uDUT__DOT__LongHierPath__unnamedblk1_1__DOT____Vrepeat0 = VL_RAND_RESET_I(32);
      |         ^~~~~~
VXX_YY_test___024root__DepSet_hc51b8287__0.cpp: In member function ‘VlCoroutine VXX_YY_test___024root::__VnoInFunc_XX_YY_test__DOT__UserCsrRead32_TOP(VXX_YY_test__Syms*, IData, IData&, IData)’:
VXX_YY_test___024root__DepSet_hc51b8287__0.cpp:1416:9: error: ‘vlSelf’ was not declared in this scope
 1416 |         vlSelf->__Vtask_XX_YY_test__DOT__uDUT__DOT__LongHierPath__unnamedblk1_4__DOT____Vrepeat3 = VL_RAND_RESET_I(32);
      |         ^~~~~~
